message:
feat(version): add multi-source version detection with graceful fallback

Implements a robust version detection system that supports three sources
with automatic fallback:

1. Build-time ldflags (highest priority):
   - GoReleaser: -X .../version.version=v{{.Version}}
   - Nix flake: Same pattern with ${version} substitution
   - Manual builds: go build -ldflags "-X .../version.version=vX.Y.Z"

2. Module version from debug.ReadBuildInfo():
   - Automatically populated when users run "go install ...@vX.Y.Z"
   - Extracts Main.Version from embedded module info
   - Normalizes by adding "v" prefix if missing

3. Hardcoded fallback (const fallback = "v0.14.4"):
   - Last resort when neither ldflags nor build info available
   - Manually bumped alongside release tags

Implementation details:
- Changed ldflags target from .Version (exported) to .version (private)
  so init() can detect whether injection occurred
- Updated .goreleaser.yaml and flake.nix to use lowercase .version
- init() runs on package load and sets the exported Version var

This allows "go install github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer@latest"
to work correctly without custom build scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ builds:
     binary: bv
     ldflags:
       - -s -w
-      - -X github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pkg/version.Version=v{{.Version}}
+      - -X github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pkg/version.version=v{{.Version}}
     goos:
       - linux
       - darwin

@@ -1,7 +1,48 @@
 package version
 
-// Version is the current application version.
-// This is a var (not const) so it can be overridden at build time via:
+import "runtime/debug"
+
+// version is set at build time by GoReleaser or manual ldflags:
+//
+//	go build -ldflags "-X github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pkg/version.version=v1.2.3"
 //
-//	go build -ldflags "-X github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pkg/version.Version=v1.2.3"
-var Version = "v0.14.4"
+// It starts empty so init() can distinguish "ldflags set it" from "no injection".
+var version string
+
+// fallback is the hardcoded version kept in sync with the latest release tag.
+// Used only when both ldflags and debug.ReadBuildInfo fail to provide a version.
+const fallback = "v0.14.4"
+
+// Version is the resolved application version, populated by init().
+var Version string
+
+func init() {
+	switch {
+	case version != "":
+		// 1. Build-time ldflags injection (GoReleaser, Nix, manual).
+		Version = version
+	case versionFromBuildInfo() != "":
+		// 2. Module version from "go install ...@vX.Y.Z".
+		Version = versionFromBuildInfo()
+	default:
+		// 3. Hardcoded fallback (always available, manually bumped per release).
+		Version = fallback
+	}
+}
+
+// versionFromBuildInfo extracts the module version stamped by the Go toolchain
+// when the binary is built via "go install". Returns empty string if unavailable.
+func versionFromBuildInfo() string {
+	info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()
+	if !ok {
+		return ""
+	}
+	v := info.Main.Version
+	if v == "" || v == "(devel)" {
+		return ""
+	}
+	if v[0] != 'v' {
+		v = "v" + v
+	}
+	return v
+}
